design_decisions.thmsmith_propose_topic: New flagship topic: exact non-equivalence frontier among three named invariance-based causal estimators in linear Gaussian SEMs with anchors and environments: Invariant Causal Prediction by Peters, Buhlmann, and Meinshausen 2016, Anchor Regression by Rothenhausler, Meinshausen, Buhlmann, and Peters 2021 JRSSB, and Causal Dantzig by Rothenhausler, Buhlmann, and Meinshausen 2019. Pre-anchor check: closest published theorem is Anchor Regression Section 2, which shows the population anchor path interpolates between partialling-out, OLS, and IV and gives robustness under shift perturbations; closest adjacent anchors are ICP invariant conditional-distribution parent-set theorem and Causal Dantzig linear residual-invariance equations under additive interventions. Our theorem is not that because the focal object is a finite covariance-level conflict certificate comparing the three independently defined procedures on the same SEM: derive from their published equations an explicit anchor-shift covariance pencil K(Sigma_XYA), characterize the sharp rank and kernel frontier where ICP accepted sets, anchor-regression gamma-infinity coefficients, and causal-Dantzig coefficients coincide, and exhibit a 2-anchor by 2-covariate Gaussian SEM witness where pairwise residual moment invariance holds but conditional-distribution invariance fails and the converse. If the proposal reduces to generic projection algebra, k-class or IV restatement, ordinary ICP sufficiency, or defining K as the disagreement itself, pivot or stop early. Require a concrete nonroutine object: an entrywise hand-derived K matrix from named estimating equations, a non-equivalence witness class with all covariances shown, and an iff frontier not equivalent to LP duality, support coverage, path derivatives, or definition-unfolding.

proposed_from.topic: New flagship topic: exact non-equivalence frontier among three named invariance-based causal estimators in linear Gaussian SEMs with anchors and environments: Invariant Causal Prediction by Peters, Buhlmann, and Meinshausen 2016, Anchor Regression by Rothenhausler, Meinshausen, Buhlmann, and Peters 2021 JRSSB, and Causal Dantzig by Rothenhausler, Buhlmann, and Meinshausen 2019. Pre-anchor check: closest published theorem is Anchor Regression Section 2, which shows the population anchor path interpolates between partialling-out, OLS, and IV and gives robustness under shift perturbations; closest adjacent anchors are ICP invariant conditional-distribution parent-set theorem and Causal Dantzig linear residual-invariance equations under additive interventions. Our theorem is not that because the focal object is a finite covariance-level conflict certificate comparing the three independently defined procedures on the same SEM: derive from their published equations an explicit anchor-shift covariance pencil K(Sigma_XYA), characterize the sharp rank and kernel frontier where ICP accepted sets, anchor-regression gamma-infinity coefficients, and causal-Dantzig coefficients coincide, and exhibit a 2-anchor by 2-covariate Gaussian SEM witness where pairwise residual moment invariance holds but conditional-distribution invariance fails and the converse. If the proposal reduces to generic projection algebra, k-class or IV restatement, ordinary ICP sufficiency, or defining K as the disagreement itself, pivot or stop early. Require a concrete nonroutine object: an entrywise hand-derived K matrix from named estimating equations, a non-equivalence witness class with all covariances shown, and an iff frontier not equivalent to LP duality, support coverage, path derivatives, or definition-unfolding.
proposed_from.novelty_justification: {"repo_axis":"Closest in-catalogue prior art remains the Stage -1.1 mined eid_dml_orth_uniform triple-frontier attempt and eid_lp_svar_nonequiv low-dimensional non-equivalence attempt. This revision keeps the qid in ExactID and differs by constructing an anchor/ICP/Dantzig affine covariance operator with explicit image and kernel objects, rather than a generic projection-gap or LP/SVAR pasting claim.","published_axis":"Published comparators define the three procedures separately: ICP conditional-law acceptance, anchor gamma-infinity normal equations, and Causal-Dantzig residual moments. The revised gap is the finite covariance-level frontier for when these independently defined equations share a supported coefficient, plus concrete q=p=2 covariance strata where residual invariance and conditional-law invariance separate."}
seed_list: Derive a primitive covariance pencil K(Sigma_XYA) whose rank/kernel zero set is exactly the three-way agreement frontier among ICP, anchor gamma-infinity, and Causal-Dantzig outputs.
Characterize the sharp covariance rank condition under which anchor gamma-infinity and Causal-Dantzig solution fibers are identical, including invalid-anchor and nonunique regimes.
Classify minimal 2-anchor by 2-covariate Gaussian SEM witnesses separating residual-moment invariance from ICP conditional-law invariance in both directions.
Characterize the support frontier where ICP accepted sets, anchor-regression support, and Causal-Dantzig solution supports agree, strictly include one another, or disagree.
Identify the maximal finite shift cone preserving three-way agreement beyond anchor-regression span perturbations.
Construct a covariance-level null projection-gap certificate replacing estimator-equality checks for any triple of invariance estimators in linear Gaussian SEMs.
Give a rank-change boundary theorem for weak-anchor regimes where anchor gamma-infinity remains set-valued but Causal Dantzig has multiple feasible coefficients.
Derive a distributional-residual augmentation of K that detects when first-moment anchor stability masks ICP conditional-law failure.
Give a semi-algebraic decision procedure for pairwise inclusion among ICP, anchor, and Dantzig support outputs in q=p=2 Gaussian SEMs.
Prove a perturbation-stability theorem for K under small covariance shifts, separating robust agreement from accidental equality.

seed_details.one_line: Derive a primitive covariance pencil K(Sigma_XYA) whose rank/kernel zero set is exactly the three-way agreement frontier among ICP, anchor gamma-infinity, and Causal-Dantzig outputs.

anchor_paper.author: Peters, Buhlmann, Meinshausen; Rothenhausler et al.
anchor_paper.claim: Three published procedures define distinct invariance equations but no shared covariance conflict certificate.
anchor_paper.location: ICP Sec. 2.1 eqs. (1)-(2); Anchor Sec. 2 eqs. (2.3)-(2.6); Causal Dantzig Secs. 1-2
anchor_paper.verbatim_quote: different notion of invariance, namely inner-product invariance

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Counterexample/frontier seed is stronger because K is built from named estimating equations, not estimator disagreement.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from anchor regression k-class algebra because gamma-infinity and IV already define the same endpoint.
seed_details.rebuttal: The ICP conditional-law block and Causal-Dantzig feasibility block live in different equation spaces; the rank/kernel frontier compares all three without solving them first.
seed_details.strict_delta: This is not Anchor Regression Sec. 2 because the focal object is a cross-procedure covariance pencil, not the anchor path or its IV endpoint.
seed_details.routine_collapse_check: PASS: routine projection algebra is used only after defining a new primitive K with rank-change and support-pasting clauses.

adversarial_referee.instance: K could be just a stacked disagreement vector.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Rebuttal: K is derived entrywise from published equations and includes image/kernel boundary clauses.

adversarial_referee.instance: The converse on rank-change strata may be underspecified.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Rebuttal: the conjecture names common-image and kernel-containment boundary conditions to prove necessity.

adversarial_referee.instance: Causal Dantzig may not equal the simplified C_A b=d_A equation under hidden variables.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Rebuttal: draft labels this as the population additive-anchor specialization and flags this as a proof risk.

seed_details.one_line: Characterize the sharp covariance rank condition under which anchor gamma-infinity and Causal-Dantzig solution fibers are identical, including invalid-anchor and nonunique regimes.

anchor_paper.author: Rothenhausler, Meinshausen, Buhlmann, Peters; Rothenhausler, Buhlmann, Meinshausen
anchor_paper.claim: Anchor regression and Causal Dantzig use related linear equations but attach different intervention and identification claims.
anchor_paper.verbatim_quote: Anchor regression trades off predictability and invariance

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Weakens/compares assumptions but stronger because invalid anchors and nonunique solution fibers create rank-change regimes.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from IV normal equations because both methods impose residual orthogonality to anchors.
seed_details.rebuttal: The seed targets exact equality of solution fibers under rank deficiency, not equality of one full-rank IV coefficient.
seed_details.strict_delta: This is not Causal Dantzig because the theorem compares solution fibers with anchor gamma-infinity under invalid anchors.
seed_details.routine_collapse_check: PASS: rank-change fiber equality is the focal object, not a new-domain reuse of IV algebra.

adversarial_referee.instance: May prove only sufficient full-rank conditions.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Rebuttal: seed requires separate singleton and non-singleton iff clauses.

adversarial_referee.instance: Could also classify ICP, not only AR-CD.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Rebuttal: this is a narrower backup seed; top seed handles the three-way family.

adversarial_referee.instance: Ha and Long may already give GMM equivalence.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Rebuttal: gaps note they do not give anchor-path equality criteria.

seed_details.one_line: Classify minimal 2-anchor by 2-covariate Gaussian SEM witnesses separating residual-moment invariance from ICP conditional-law invariance in both directions.

anchor_paper.author: Peters, Buhlmann, Meinshausen; Rothenhausler, Buhlmann, Meinshausen; Kook, Sick, Buhlmann
anchor_paper.venue: JRSSB / Annals of Statistics / Statistics and Computing
anchor_paper.claim: Conditional-law invariance and residual-moment invariance are conceptually distinct, but no smallest covariance catalogue is given.
anchor_paper.location: ICP Sec. 2.1; Causal Dantzig Sec. 1; Distributional Anchor Sec. 3.1
anchor_paper.verbatim_quote: does not state any invariance properties for higher moments or the entire residual distribution

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Witness seed is stronger if promoted to open semi-algebraic classes, not a hand-tuned single law.
seed_details.referee_objection: In Gaussian models moments determine distributions, so residual moments and conditional laws cannot separate.
seed_details.rebuttal: The moments condition residuals on anchors after projection; ICP conditions Y on selected covariates across environments.
seed_details.strict_delta: This is not ICP sufficiency because it gives two-sided non-equivalence witnesses between named invariance criteria.
seed_details.routine_collapse_check: PASS: witness classes and determinant minimality are focal objects beyond projection algebra.

adversarial_referee.instance: The 2-by-2 witness may sit on a zero-variance or collapsed-support corner.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Rebuttal: seed requires open semi-algebraic regions with positive covariance determinants.

adversarial_referee.instance: A witness alone may not be a flagship object.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Rebuttal: minimal determinant catalogue promotes it to a generic-class sharpness result.

adversarial_referee.instance: Distributional anchor regression may already separate full residual law from moments.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Rebuttal: it does not classify ICP-vs-Dantzig covariance witnesses in the same SEM.

seed_details.one_line: Characterize the support frontier where ICP accepted sets, anchor-regression support, and Causal-Dantzig solution supports agree, strictly include one another, or disagree.

anchor_paper.author: Peters, Buhlmann, Meinshausen; Rothenhausler, Meinshausen, Buhlmann, Peters; Heinze-Deml, Peters, Meinshausen
anchor_paper.venue: JRSSB / JRSSB / Journal of Causal Inference
anchor_paper.claim: ICP returns accepted invariant sets while anchor regression gives coefficient paths and support-like screening.
anchor_paper.location: ICP Sec. 2; Anchor Sec. 2; Nonlinear ICP Sec. 3
anchor_paper.verbatim_quote: accepted-set behavior and power can depend strongly on causal structure

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Target variation stronger because support comparison reveals an ID gap hidden by coefficient comparison.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is just thresholding coefficients after solving each method.
seed_details.rebuttal: The intended theorem gives zero-pattern implications and rank conditions before solving the procedures.
seed_details.strict_delta: This is not Heinze-Deml et al. because it compares three support notions by covariance strata.
seed_details.routine_collapse_check: lands; downgrade to field-tier unless paired with K frontier.

seed_details.one_line: Identify the maximal finite shift cone preserving three-way agreement beyond anchor-regression span perturbations.

anchor_paper.author: Rothenhausler, Meinshausen, Buhlmann, Peters; Henzi, Shen, Law, Buhlmann; Shen, Buhlmann, Taeb
anchor_paper.claim: New robustness papers broaden perturbation classes without analyzing three-method equality frontiers.
anchor_paper.location: Anchor Sec. 3; IPP robustness sections; DRIG main theorem sections
anchor_paper.verbatim_quote: arbitrary shifts need not admit invariant robust probabilistic predictions

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Boundary/regime shift stronger if it gives a maximal cone and counterexample outside the cone.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from anchor regression's anchor-span robustness theorem.
seed_details.rebuttal: The seed targets three-way agreement beyond the anchor span, including ICP and Dantzig outputs.
seed_details.strict_delta: This is not Anchor Regression robustness because the shift cone preserves cross-method agreement, not risk robustness alone.
seed_details.routine_collapse_check: PASS if maximal cone is computed from K; otherwise downgrade.

adversarial_referee.instance: DRIG may already cover the broader shift class.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Rebuttal: DRIG broadens robustness, not the ICP-AR-CD agreement frontier.

adversarial_referee.instance: Needs a computable cone, not qualitative robustness.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Rebuttal: seed requires finite covariance perturbation cone and outside-cone counterexample.

adversarial_referee.instance: May only prove agreement is preserved inside a cone.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Rebuttal: maximality and outside-cone witness are required.

seed_details.one_line: Construct a covariance-level null projection-gap certificate replacing estimator-equality checks for any triple of invariance estimators in linear Gaussian SEMs.

anchor_paper.author: prior CausalSmith reviewer signal
anchor_paper.claim: Prior triple-frontier attempt was downgraded for lacking an explicit null projection-gap condition.
anchor_paper.verbatim_quote: replacing span equality with an explicit null pairwise projection-gap condition

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Bridge seed stronger because it uses a prior reviewer gap as a primitive object requirement.
seed_details.referee_objection: This abstracts away from the named ICP, anchor, and Dantzig papers.
seed_details.rebuttal: It is a backup formulation; flagship version anchors K to the three named published equations.
seed_details.strict_delta: This is not eid_dml_orth_uniform because the projection-gap certificate is covariance-level for SEM invariance equations.
seed_details.routine_collapse_check: lands; downgrade to field-tier unless specialized to named estimators.

seed_details.one_line: Give a rank-change boundary theorem for weak-anchor regimes where anchor gamma-infinity remains set-valued but Causal Dantzig has multiple feasible coefficients.

anchor_paper.author: Rothenhausler, Meinshausen, Buhlmann, Peters; prior proximal proposal
anchor_paper.claim: Weak or invalid anchors create solution-fiber and first-order equivalence issues.
anchor_paper.verbatim_quote: the main open work is proving the tangent equivalence and guarding rank-change boundary cases

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Boundary seed stronger because it isolates rank-change regimes rather than broadening assumptions.
seed_details.referee_objection: Rank-change cases are measure-zero algebraic pathology.
seed_details.rebuttal: Weak-anchor regimes are named in anchor regression and govern nonunique solution fibers, so boundary behavior affects inference and support comparison.
seed_details.strict_delta: This is not a generic rank-deficiency note because it compares two named solution fibers under invalid anchors.
seed_details.routine_collapse_check: PASS: boundary fiber theorem creates a new frontier object.

adversarial_referee.instance: Could be too narrow as boundary-only algebra.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Rebuttal: use as clause inside the three-way K frontier, not standalone headline.

adversarial_referee.instance: Rank deficiency may require zero anchor covariance.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Rebuttal: require nonzero weak-anchor covariance and locally described strata.
Rebuttal: backup seed; top seed names the joint family.

seed_details.one_line: Derive a distributional-residual augmentation of K that detects when first-moment anchor stability masks ICP conditional-law failure.

anchor_paper.author: Kook, Sick, Buhlmann; Peters, Buhlmann, Meinshausen
anchor_paper.claim: Distributional anchor regression separates first-moment residual invariance from distributional invariance.
anchor_paper.verbatim_quote: aspects of the residual distribution remain invariant

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Extends residual invariance stronger only if the augmentation changes the frontier rather than restating full distributional ICP.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is just distributional anchor regression applied to ICP.
seed_details.rebuttal: The proposed object augments K to compare three named procedures on the same covariance/conditional-law SEM.
seed_details.strict_delta: This is not Kook et al. because it embeds distributional residual checks in a three-method conflict certificate.
seed_details.routine_collapse_check: lands; downgrade to field-tier unless a computable finite Gaussian augmentation is explicit.

seed_details.one_line: Give a semi-algebraic decision procedure for pairwise inclusion among ICP, anchor, and Dantzig support outputs in q=p=2 Gaussian SEMs.

anchor_paper.author: prior LP/SVAR proposal; Peters, Buhlmann, Meinshausen
anchor_paper.claim: Prior reviewer flagged finite Gram-determinant descriptions in dimension two as open.
anchor_paper.verbatim_quote: the finite Gram-determinant description in dimension two are the main open pieces

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Algorithmic-identifiability seed stronger if it provides a decision procedure with determinant conditions.
seed_details.referee_objection: Dimension-two classification is a toy calculation.
seed_details.rebuttal: It is useful as the explicit witness and minimality engine for the flagship K frontier.
seed_details.strict_delta: This is not eid_lp_svar_nonequiv because the procedures are ICP, anchor regression, and Causal Dantzig.
seed_details.routine_collapse_check: lands; downgrade to field-tier as standalone; useful as support theorem for top seed.

seed_details.one_line: Prove a perturbation-stability theorem for K under small covariance shifts, separating robust agreement from accidental equality.

anchor_paper.author: Rothenhausler, Meinshausen, Buhlmann, Peters; Henzi, Shen, Law, Buhlmann
anchor_paper.claim: Anchor robustness is shift-span based, while newer invariant prediction studies broader perturbations.
anchor_paper.location: Anchor robustness theorem sections; IPP main robustness discussion
anchor_paper.verbatim_quote: robustness guarantees for shift interventions lying in an anchor-generated span

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Sensitivity seed stronger if it computes a critical perturbation radius from K's smallest singular value.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is standard matrix perturbation theory once K is defined.
seed_details.rebuttal: As standalone it is routine; as a corollary it quantifies accidental equality boundaries of the new K object.
seed_details.strict_delta: This is not Anchor Regression because it studies stability of cross-method agreement, not prediction loss alone.
seed_details.routine_collapse_check: lands; downgrade to field-tier unless tied to a critical singular-value frontier.

proposed_from.literature_map:
Peters, Buhlmann, and Meinshausen (2016, JRSSB) define ICP through equality of conditional distributions Y|X_S across environments and derive conservative accepted sets.
Rothenhausler, Buhlmann, and Meinshausen (2019, Annals of Statistics) introduce Causal Dantzig as an inner-product invariance method for linear SEMs with hidden variables under additive interventions.
Rothenhausler, Meinshausen, Buhlmann, and Peters (2021, JRSSB) introduce anchor regression, whose population path interpolates among partialling-out, OLS, and IV/TSLS.
Anchor regression allows invalid anchors and provides robustness under shift perturbations, but then causal parameters need not be identifiable.
Kook, Sick, and Buhlmann (2022, Statistics and Computing) generalize anchor ideas to distributional anchor regression and separate first-moment residual invariance from distributional residual invariance.
Ha and Long (2023) recast Causal-Dantzig and environment constraints in a GMM/instrument framework, giving algebraic proximity to IV moments but not a three-estimator equivalence frontier.
Heinze-Deml, Peters, and Meinshausen (2018, Journal of Causal Inference) show accepted-set behavior can depend strongly on model structure and power.
Henzi, Shen, Law, and Buhlmann (2025, Biometrika) move from point prediction to probabilistic prediction and note that arbitrary shifts do not generally admit invariant robust probabilistic predictions.
Shen, Buhlmann, and Taeb (2026, JASA) generalize anchor regression through invariant gradients or noise interventions, widening the perturbation-class tension.
No located paper appears to solve the finite covariance-level conflict certificate or sharp rank/kernel frontier among ICP, anchor gamma-infinity, and Causal Dantzig.

banked_reason: Stopped at D-0.5 after angle0 v2 remained field-tier under flagship target: Theorem 1 was already-known population translation of ICP/anchor-regression/Causal-Dantzig equations; Conjecture 1 reduced to definitional affine-image stacking; Causal-Dantzig anchor-moment bridge was missing; flagship upgrade would require an independent determinant/minor classification and minimality theorem.
